seo.title: Trat Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Trat travel guide for 2026. 4-day itinerary covering Koh Chang beaches, waterfalls, and island hopping to Koh Mak and Koh Kood. Transport from Bangkok, seafood guide, budget breakdown.

intro: Trat province is Thailand's eastern island gateway — a world away from the Andaman coast crowds. Four days is the sweet spot: enough for Koh Chang's beaches and waterfalls, a day trip to the outer islands, and an evening exploring Trat's atmospheric old town. The province delivers genuine island beauty, incredible seafood, and Thailand's best tropical fruit at a fraction of Phuket or Koh Samui prices. This guide covers everything you need to plan the trip — transport from Bangkok, island ferry schedules, food highlights, and realistic budget breakdowns.

itinerary.title: 4 Days in Trat: Beaches, Waterfalls & Island Hopping

days.title: Trat Town & Ferry to Koh Chang
activities: Morning: Arrive in Trat by bus from Bangkok (Ekkamai terminal, 5-6 hours, 250-350 THB) or flight to Trat Airport (TDX, 1 hour, from 2,500 THB)
Late morning: If time allows, walk Trat Old Town — explore the narrow riverside lanes and century-old wooden shophouses
Midday: Take a songthaew to Ao Thammachat pier and catch the car ferry to Koh Chang (every 45 min, 30-minute crossing, 80 THB)
Afternoon: Check into your hotel on White Sand Beach or Klong Prao — rent a scooter (250-300 THB/day) and explore the west coast
Sunset: Head to Kai Bae viewpoint for panoramic views over the offshore islands
Evening: Seafood dinner at a beachfront restaurant — grilled red snapper with salt crust is the local specialty (300-600 THB)

days.title: Koh Chang Waterfalls & Beaches
activities: Early morning: Head to Klong Plu Waterfall before the crowds (opens 8 AM, 200 THB national park fee) — swim in the natural pool at the base
Mid-morning: Drive across the island to Than Mayom Waterfall on the east coast (same-day park ticket valid) — look for King Rama V's royal initials on the rocks
Lunch: Eat at a local restaurant in the east coast fishing village of Salak Phet — fresh crab and squid at local prices (80-150 THB)
Afternoon: Head back to the west coast and spend the afternoon at Klong Prao Beach — the longest and most peaceful beach on the island
Late afternoon: Optional — kayak through the mangrove forest at Salak Phet (800-1,500 THB guided tour) or explore the Klong Prao estuary
Evening: Walk the Bang Bao fishing village boardwalk at sunset, then dinner at one of the stilt-built seafood restaurants

days.title: Island Hopping — Koh Mak & Koh Kood
activities: Morning: Take the Boonsiri Ferry from Bang Bao pier at 10:00 — arrives Koh Mak at 11:00 (450-750 THB one-way)
Late morning: Rent a bicycle (50-100 THB) and explore Koh Mak — cycle past coconut plantations to Ao Suan Yai beach
Lunch: Fresh seafood at a beachside restaurant on Koh Mak (100-300 THB)
Option A: Stay on Koh Mak for the afternoon — snorkel off the southern beach, then catch sunset at Ao Suan Yai before taking the afternoon ferry back to Koh Chang

Option B: Continue to Koh Kood on the 11:00 ferry (arrives 11:45) — spend the afternoon at Klong Chao Beach and swim at Klong Chao Waterfall. Return ferry departs 12:15 back to Koh Chang (arrives 14:00)
Evening: Return to Koh Chang — relaxed dinner and drinks at Lonely Beach bars

days.title: Snorkeling, Diving or Beach Day
activities: Option A — Snorkeling: Full-day snorkeling tour to Koh Rang marine park (800-1,500 THB plus 200 THB park fee) — the best coral reefs in the Gulf of Thailand
Option A afternoon: Return to Koh Chang around 3-4 PM, final beach time at White Sand Beach
Option B — Diving: Fun dive at Koh Rang for certified divers (2,500-3,500 THB for two dives) or start day 1 of a PADI Open Water course (12,000-15,000 THB total)
Option C — Beach Day: Spend the morning at your favorite beach, try a Thai cooking class (1,000-1,800 THB), or visit the Treetop Adventure Park (800-1,200 THB)
Afternoon: Last souvenir shopping at White Sand Beach or Klong Prao
Evening: Final sunset seafood BBQ on the beach before catching the evening ferry back to the mainland

transport.fromBangkok: Bus from Ekkamai (Eastern) Bus Terminal — departures every 30-60 minutes from 05:00 to 23:00, 5-6 hours, 250-350 THB (~$7-10 USD). The most popular and cheapest option. Bangkok Airways flights from Suvarnabhumi (BKK) to Trat (TDX) — about 1 hour, from 2,500 THB one-way, up to 4 daily flights. From Trat, songthaews wait at the airport to take passengers to the ferry pier (280 THB per person including ferry ticket). The car ferry from Ao Thammachat pier to Koh Chang runs every 45 minutes from 06:30-18:30, takes 30 minutes, costs 80 THB per person. For Koh Kood and Koh Mak, speedboats depart from Laem Sok pier (Koh Kood, 1.5 hrs, 600-1,200 THB) and Laem Ngop pier (Koh Mak, 1 hr, 450-750 THB).
localTransport: Car ferry to Koh Chang: Every 45 minutes 06:30-18:30 from Ao Thammachat pier, 30-minute crossing, 80 THB per person, 120 THB per car. No advance booking needed
Boonsiri Ferry (inter-island): Koh Chang (Bang Bao) → Koh Mak (1 hr) → Koh Kood (1.75 hrs), 750-1,200 THB. High season only (Nov-May), reduced service in monsoon
Scooter rental on Koh Chang: 250-300 THB/day — strongly recommended, the island is large and songthaews are infrequent
Songthaews on Koh Chang: Shared pickup trucks along the west coast road, 50-100 THB per trip. Infrequent — don't rely on them for timed activities
Bicycles on Koh Mak: 50-100 THB/day — the island is flat and small enough to cycle in an hour. The best way to explore
Scooter on Koh Kood: 300 THB/day — the only practical transport, no public transport exists
Trat airport transfers: Songthaew to Ao Thammachat ferry pier 280 THB per person (includes ferry ticket to Koh Chang)

mustTry: Grilled whole red snapper (pla krapong) with salt crust — the signature Koh Chang dish, fresh-caught and grilled over charcoal (200-400 THB)
Gaeng som pla (sour fish curry) — Trat's local specialty, a tangy-spicy soup with fresh fish and vegetables (60-100 THB)
Grilled squid and prawn skewers — street food staple at Trat Night Market, incredibly fresh (30-60 THB per skewer)
Durian — Trat is Thailand's durian capital. Try fresh Monthong variety from roadside stalls (100-300 THB per portion). Season: April-June
Mangosteen — the 'queen of fruits' grows abundantly in Trat province. Deep purple shell with sweet, white flesh (40-80 THB per kilo in season)
Pad cha (stir-fried seafood with wild ginger) — a spicy seafood stir-fry with rhizome, peppercorns, and krachai. More common in eastern Thailand (100-200 THB)
Fresh coconut ice cream — sold at beach stalls and night markets, served in the coconut shell (40-60 THB)
foodAreas: Trat Night Market (Tha Ruea Road, nightly 5-9 PM) — the best street food concentration at genuine local prices. Grilled seafood, curries, fruit shakes
Bang Bao boardwalk (Koh Chang) — stilt-built restaurants over the water with fresh seafood. Touristy but the setting is unique
White Sand Beach restaurants (Koh Chang) — the widest selection from cheap Thai to international. Beachfront seafood BBQ at sunset
Klong Prao riverside restaurants (Koh Chang) — quieter than White Sand Beach with good Thai food at fair prices
Salak Phet fishing village (Koh Chang east coast) — the most authentic and cheapest seafood on the island, eaten where the boats come in
tips: Seafood restaurants price by weight (per 100g) — always ask for the total weight and price before ordering to avoid bill shock
Trat Night Market food is 50-70% cheaper than Koh Chang restaurant prices — eat here if you're overnighting in town
Durian is banned from most hotels and ferries due to the smell — eat it at the stall where you buy it
Koh Kood has very few restaurants — most visitors eat at their resort. Expect to pay 150-300 THB per dish
Mangosteen season (May-July) overlaps with durian — try both at Trat's roadside fruit stalls for the freshest taste

etiquette: Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples on Koh Chang — there are several small temples on the island
Remove shoes before entering any temple building or Thai home
Don't point your feet at people or Buddha images when sitting
Tipping is not expected but appreciated — 20-50 THB for restaurant meals, 50-100 THB for boat drivers and guides
Stand during the national anthem played at 8 AM and 6 PM in public areas
Respect the marine environment — don't step on coral reefs, use reef-safe sunscreen, don't feed fish

budget.budget: 600-1,200 THB/day (~$17-34 USD): Lonely Beach bungalow 500-800 THB, street food and local restaurants 150-300 THB, scooter rental 250 THB, free beach days and waterfall visits (200 THB park fee). Koh Chang is significantly cheaper than Phuket or Koh Samui — a genuine budget destination.
budget.midRange: 2,000-4,500 THB/day (~$56-126 USD): Klong Prao or Kai Bae mid-range resort 1,500-3,500 THB, mix of beach restaurants and local food 500-1,000 THB, one organized tour (snorkeling 800-1,500 THB, cooking class 1,000-1,800 THB), scooter 250 THB. The sweet spot for most visitors.
budget.luxury: 8,000-40,000+ THB/day (~$224-1,120+ USD): KC Grande or Santhiya on Koh Chang 4,000-12,000 THB, or Soneva Kiri on Koh Kood 35,000+ THB. Fine dining 1,500-3,000 THB, private boat tours 5,000-10,000 THB, spa treatments 2,000-5,000 THB. Soneva Kiri is one of Thailand's most exclusive resorts.
